content: TIA treatment focuses on urgent evaluation and secondary prevention. Immediate management: aspirin 160-325mg ASAP (reduces early stroke risk by ~80%). Dual antiplatelet therapy (DAPT): clopidogrel 75mg daily PLUS aspirin 81mg daily for 21 days (CHANCE/POINT trials—reduces 90-day stroke risk by ~25% in high-risk TIA/minor stroke), then clopidogrel monotherapy. For atrial fibrillation: anticoagulation with DOAC (apixaban, rivaroxaban, dabigatran, edoxaban) instead of antiplatelet; timing depends on infarct size (immediate for TIA, delayed 3-14 days for stroke to reduce hemorrhagic conversion risk). High-intensity statin (atorvastatin 80mg or rosuvastatin 20-40mg) regardless of LDL. BP control target <130/80 long-term. Carotid revascularization: symptomatic carotid stenosis 70-99% benefits from carotid endarterectomy (CEA) within 2 weeks (NASCET trial); 50-69% modest benefit in men; <50% no benefit. Carotid stenting alternative for high surgical risk. Intracranial stenosis: aggressive medical management (DAPT × 90 days, intensive risk factor control) preferred over stenting (SAMMPRIS trial). Lifestyle: smoking cessation, Mediterranean diet, exercise, weight management.
